info.title: eye.do - Integrations API
info.description:
This API is for interacting with eyedo by integrating systems.  

All `id`s represent `id`s of the integrating system,
not the internal `id`.   
Each `id` can be an
integer or a simple, short string, up to 16 characters.


The URL for accessing the API is **http(s)://ext.api.eye.do**.  
An `Authorization:` header must begin with "`Token`" and
then the api key.   

Note that the namings in this API may be
different than what you are used to. This is because terms are localized for
your organization, but not in the api.  

Field queries: In
development stages you could use `fields=*` to query and
investigate all possible fields. **Do not use `*` in
production**, as this may introduce incompatible changes, or give you
megabytes of extra data as fields are added.  

Documentation for
`v4` could be found [here](https://www.eye.do/apidocs/ext/v4).  
Documentation for `v3` could be found
[here](https://www.eye.do/apidocs/ext/v3).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit results count

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Get the next results for a query, based on the `after` token supplied

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Get the previous results for a query, based on the `before` token supplied

post.summary: Create or update multiple `tag` items
post.description:
• id field is required.
  • If the id of an item does not exist, a new item with this id will be created.
  • It is also possible to send a single item without wrapping in an array. In such a case, the response will also not be wrapped in an array.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the item to be fetched

post.summary:
Update a specific `tag`.
You can specify one or more of the described parameters.
post.description:
• All fields are optional.
  • It is possible to change the id of an item, by specifying an id in the body.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the item to be updated or created

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the item to be deleted

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit results count

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Get the next results for a query, based on the `after` token supplied

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Get the previous results for a query, based on the `before` token supplied

parameters.description: Determines which fields to fetch

parameters.name: last_change_after
parameters.description: Fetch all tasks who were created/updated after this timestamp. Use `last_update_time` field to determine the value for next fetch.

parameters.name: created_on_from
parameters.description: Determines a start date for filtering (by creation date). You can specify either `last_change_after` or `created_on_from`+`created_on_to` or `date_from`+`date_to`

parameters.name: created_on_to
parameters.description: Determines an end date for filtering (by creation date). You can specify either `last_change_after` or `created_on_from`+`created_on_to` or `date_from`+`date_to`

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Determines a task start date for filtering. You can specify either `last_change_after` or `created_on_from`+`created_on_to` or `date_from`+`date_to`

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Determines a task end date for filtering. You can specify either `last_change_after` or `created_on_from`+`created_on_to` or `date_from`+`date_to`

get.summary: Fetch agent reports data by report date

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Determines which fields to fetch

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Determines a start date for filtering. You can specify either `last_change_after` or `date_from`+`date_to`

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Determines an end date for filtering. You can specify either `last_change_after` or `date_from`+`date_to`

parameters.name: last_change_after
parameters.description: Fetch all reports who were created/updated after this timestamp. Use `last_change_after` field to determine the value for next fetch. You can specify either `last_change_after` or `date_from`+`date_to`.

parameters.name: consider_questionnaire_last_change
parameters.description: Determines whether to refer to the last update date of the questionnaire. Additional parameter for last_change_after

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status, or multiple statuses (comma separated)

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Filter by a task id, or multiple task ids (comma separated)

parameters.name: sector_id
parameters.description: Filter by a sector id, or multiple sector ids (comma separated)

parameters.name: summary_for_visit_id
parameters.description: Filter by visit summary for specific visit id

parameters.description: Get the previous results for a query, based on the `before` token supplied

parameters.description: ID of the agent report

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: File name

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the agent report

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Determines what type of files to delete. Default: `all`

Identifier.description: An identifier can be an `Int64` or a `string[16]`

is_active.description: When a `entity` is inactive, no tasks can be performed in it.

entity_map.description: A mapping of associated `entity` items, by type.

agent_map.description: A list of agents associated with this `entity`.

user_ids.description: A list of user ids that are in charge of this `entity`.

question_ids.description: A list of question ids associated with this `entity`.

tag_ids.description: A list of tag ids associated with this entity.

tag_names.description: A list of tag names associated with this entity.

is_active.description: When a `entity` is inactive, no tasks can be performed in it.

entity_map.description: A mapping of associated `entity` items, by type.

agent_map.description: A list of agents associated with this `entity`.

agent_ids.description: A list of agent ids with this `entity`.

user_map.description: **Read only.** A list of users that are in charge of this `entity`.

user_ids.description: A list of user ids that are in charge of this `entity`.

question_map.description: **Read only.** A list of questions associated with this `entity`.

question_ids.description: A list of question ids associated with this `entity`.

tag_map.description: **Read only.** A list of tags associated with this `entity`.

tag_ids.description: A list of tag ids associated with this `entity`.

tag_names.description: A list of tag names associated with this entity.

created_on.description: Date/time when the record was created.

last_update_time.description: Date/time when the record was last updated.

show_all_agent_reports.description: Is the agent allowed to see all agent reports in this `entity`?

allow_upload_photos.description: Can the agent upload photos, or must he/she take fresh new photos?

allow_web_access.description: Can the agent access the tasks through *my eyedo*?

allow_web_send_report.description: Can the agent send reports through *my eyedo*?

allow_edit_report.description: Can the agent edit reports through?

allow_unassociated_entity.description: Can the agent see entities that are not associated to him/her?

allow_edit_profile.description: Can the agent edit his own profile? (Has effect only for domain accounts)

allow_save_visit_out_of_activity_hours.description: Can the agent save a visit with time out of activity hours?

allow_edit_custom_fields.description: Can the agent edit custom fields on entities?

allow_alerts_from_all_creators.description: Can handle alerts from other users?

show_all_agent_reports.description: Is the agent allowed to see all agent reports in this `entity`?

name.description: Name or nickname in your organization (may be different than first+last name, but synced to those by default)

is_user.description: Does this user has access to the backoffice?

is_agent.description: Does this user has access to the agent app?

is_active.description: If false, the user's access to your organization is postponed.

is_locked.description: Usually happens after too many failed logins.

password_is_initial.description: Is the user's password the initial password he/she was assigned? You may only have access to this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

user_agent.description: The user-agent this user has last logged in with

type.description: The type of this user or user-request

agent_permissions.description: If this user has `is_agent` set, then this determines their permissions as a field user.

user_permissions.description: If this user has `is_user` set, then this determines their permissions as a backoffice user.

entities.description: A list of entities that this user is associated with.

delegated_agent_ids.description: List of agents that this agent has permission to operate on their behalf. This list contains user IDs, not emails.

user_roles.description: List of roles (permission-wise) for the user. If specified - it determines the actual `account_permissions`, `sectors` and subaccounts.

user_role_ids.description: List of roles (permission-wise) for the user. If specified - it determines the actual `account_permissions`, `sectors` and subaccounts.

agent_roles.description: List of roles (permission-wise) for the user when acting as an agent. If specified - it determines the actual `agent_permissions` and `sectors`.

agent_role_ids.description: List of roles (permission-wise) for the user when acting as an agent. If specified - it determines the actual `agent_permissions` and `sectors`.

is_authorized_domain.description: Is this user's email address in the organization's verified domain?

name.description: Name or nickname in your organization (may be different than first+last name, but synced to those by default)

first_name.description: Profile first name. You can only update this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

last_name.description: Profile last name. You can only update this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

phone.description: Profile phone number. You can only update this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

default_lang_code.description: Profile default UI language. You can only update this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

gender.description: Profile gender. You can only update this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

is_user.description: Does this user has access to the backoffice?

is_agent.description: Does this user has access to the agent app?

is_active.description: If false, the user's access to your organization is postponed.

is_locked.description: Usually happens after too many failed logins. You can only update this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

password.description: You are only able to set this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain, or when creating a brand new user.

password_is_initial.description: Is the user's password the initial password he/she was assigned? You may only have access to this field if the user's email is in your organization's verified domain.

agent_permissions.description: If this user has `is_agent` set, then this determines their permissions as a field user.

user_permissions.description: If this user has `is_user` set, then this determines their permissions as a backoffice user.

user_role_ids.description: List of roles (permission-wise) for the user. If specified - it replaces the actual `account_permissions`, `sectors` and subaccounts. Sectors are merged with sectors from agent roles.

agent_role_ids.description: List of roles (permission-wise) for the user when acting as an agent. If specified - it replaces the actual `agent_permissions` and `sectors`. Sectors are merged with sectors from user roles.

stick_to_end_of_month.description:
I.e. when the event occurs on the last day the 28th, move it to the 29th/30th on the next month.
(Used for monthly recurrences only)

weekdays.description: A string of digits per day, where `0` is Sunday and `6` is Saturday.

recurrence_end.description:
When do the recurrences end.
Dates are ISO-8601. Note that these dates must include timezone information.

description.example:
Project description here
Could be multiline also.

template_id.description: Passing a `template_id` will create a project from an existing template. You will also be required to pass `name` and `start_time`.

description.example:
Project description here
Could be multiline also.

template_id.description: Passing a `template_id` will create a task from an existing template. You will need to pass at least `start_date`/`end_date`.

description.example:
Task description here
Could be multiline also.

add_description_to_answers.description: When `true`, the task description is automatically prepended to the answers array in a report.

start_date.description:
Task start date/time.
Dates are ISO-8601. Note that these dates must include timezone information.

end_date.description:
Task end date/time.
Dates are ISO-8601. Note that these dates must include timezone information.

visible_since.description:
A date/time earlier or equal to `start_date`, where the task is already visible to the agent but not reportable yet.
Dates are ISO-8601. Note that these dates must include timezone information.

visible_until.description:
A date/time later or equal to `end_date`, where the task is still visible and reportable, while the date the shows visually as the end date is still `end_date`.
Dates are ISO-8601. Note that these dates must include timezone information.

max_distance_meters.description: If a coordinate is available, it will be required to be within this radius from the destination

send_notification_out_of_account_activit.description: Send notifications also out of account operation time.

allow_edit_report.description: Allow manually editing reports of this task.

allow_web_report.description: Allow reporting this task from *my eyedo*.

block_upload_photos_from_gallery.description: Block uploading photos from a gallery.

skip_to_questionnaire.description: Allow skipping the client details screen to the questionnaire.

enable_summary_document.description: Enable creating a summary document for this task.

allow_agent_send_summary_document.description: Give the reporting agent a choice about sending the summary document.

show_answers_from_previous_report.description: Enable showing answers from previous report in a routine task.

restrict_to_agent_entities.description:
When `true`, only agents associated with the task's entities can report it.
Otherwise, all agents associated with the task can report it.

recurrence_show_answers_from_previous_re.description: Enable showing answers from previous report in task with recurrence.

agent_ids.description: Array of agents' ids (Use either `agent_ids` or `agent_emails`)

agent_emails.description: Array of agents' emails (Use either `agent_ids` or `agent_emails`)

entity_map.description: Array of the entity mappings, on which to perform the task

entity_choice_map.description: Array of the entity mappings, available to select within the task

end_date.description:

should_track_route.description: Should route tracking be enabled for this visit?

summary_doc_url.description: Url to the summary document pdf

description.example:
Visit description here
Could be multiline also.

start_date.description:
Task start date/time.
Dates are ISO-8601. Note that these dates must include timezone information.

end_date.description:
Task end date/time.
Dates are ISO-8601. Note that these dates must include timezone information.

is_created_by_agent.description: **Read only.** Determines that this visit was created by an agent.

departure_date.description: **Read only.** Date/time of when the agent's departure towards the visit.

departure_distance.description: **Read only.** How far was the agent from the visit's location on departure? Value specified in meters.

departure_latlon_accuracy.description: **Read only.** How accurate was the GPS reading on departure? Value specified in meters.

arrival_date.description: **Read only.** Date/time of when the agent's arrival to the visit's location.

arrival_distance.description: **Read only.** How far was the agent from the visit's location on arrival? Value specified in meters.

arrival_latlon_accuracy.description: **Read only.** How accurate was the GPS reading on departure? Value specified in meters.

done_date.description: **Read only.** Date/time of when the agent has pressed "done".

done_distance.description: **Read only.** How far was the agent from the visit's location on "done"? Value specified in meters.

done_latlon_accuracy.description: **Read only.** How accurate was the GPS reading on "done"? Value specified in meters.

not_done_date.description: **Read only.** Date/time of when the agent has pressed "not done".

not_done_distance.description: **Read only.** How far was the agent from the visit's location on "not done"? Value specified in meters.

not_done_latlon_accuracy.description: **Read only.** How accurate was the GPS reading on "not done"? Value specified in meters.

comment_to_manager.description: **Read only.** Comment from the agent to the manager, left for the next visit in the same location.

not_done_reason_name.description: **Read only.** The reason for "not done" action.

not_done_reason_note.description: **Read only.** An optional note for "not done" action by the agent.

entity_tags.description: **Read only.** Comma separated list of tags.

should_track_route.description: Is route tracking enabled for this visit?

last_update_time.description: Date/time when the record was last updated

images.description: image names (complex json, documentation to be done)

signature_images.description: image names (complex json, documentation to be done)

status_change_reason.example: Text entered by user who changed report status

time_estimate.description: Time estimate for the questionnaire, in seconds
Time estimate for the questionnaire, in seconds. This is the use specified estimate, not the AI generated one.

QuestionNumericValidationRuleCondition.description:
Conditions:
* `eq` - equals to  
* `lt` - less than  
* `lte` - less than or equal to  
* `gt` - greater than  
* `gte` - greater than or equal to  
* `bt` - between  
* `nbt` - not between

QuestionDateTimeAlertRuleCondition.description:
Conditions: 
* `eq` - equals to  
* `lt` - less than  
* `lte` - less than or equal to  
* `gt` - greater than  
* `gte` - greater than or equal to  
* `bt` - between  
* `nbt` - not between  
* `oobh` - outside of business hours  
* `gt_now_plus` - greater than today plus days  
* `lt_now_plus` - less to today plus days  
* `eq_now_plus` - equals to today plus days

QuestionTextValidationCondition.description:
Conditions: 
* `eq` - equals to  
* `neq` - not equals to  
* `bt` - between  
* `nbt` - not between  
* `rgx` - matches regex  
* `nrgx` - negates regex

QuestionDateTimeValidationCondition.description:
Conditions: 
* `eq` - equals to  
* `neq` - not equals to  
* `lt` - less than  
* `lte` - less than or equal to  
* `gt` - greater than  
* `gte` - greater than or equal to  
* `bt` - between  
* `nbt` - not between  
* `frm` - matches formula

oneOf.description: For 'calc' type, this is the formula. For 'project_field' type, this is id of the project field. For 'entity_field' type, this is id of the entity record field.
Complex object for 'raw' type containing question specific default structural data (answers, numeric_value, date_value etc.)

type_id.description: For 'entity_field' type, this is used to specify the entity type id.

oneOf.description: For 'text' type, this is the text value. For 'project_field' type, this is id of the project field. For 'entity_field' type, this is id of the entity record field.

type_id.description: For 'entity_field' type, this is used to specify the entity type id.

pass_rule.description: Condition to evaluate for pass_rule mode. i.e x=5, x!=5, x>5, x<5 x>=5, x<=5, x>5&&x<10, x<5||x>10

pass_score.description: Score to assign if pass_rule condition is met.

image.description: Use the `/questions/{id}/image/download?name=[image]` endpoint to fetch the image.

is_read_only.description: Answers to this question are not editable. They may have some default value (not yet supported in the api).

ignore_in_reports.description: This question is hidden in reports.

tags.name: tags
tags.description: Each project and entity can be associated with tags, and separated by a sector.

tags.name: entities
tags.description: Each entity could be of any given type, customized in the system.

tags.name: users
tags.description: Users have access to the backend at http://app.eye.do, to manage all the entities, tasks, and generate reports.

tags.name: agent_groups
tags.description: Groups for agents (a kind of user).

tags.name: projects
tags.description: Projects define are flat hierarchical parent for tasks.

tags.name: tasks
tags.description: Tasks define single or multiple tasks according to timings, entities and agents associated.

tags.name: agentreports
tags.description: This is information posted by a user in response to a task or a visit

tags.name: reports
tags.description: Reports module summarizes information posted by a user by task or visit. Allows cuts by date or additional parameters

tags.name: questionnaires
tags.description: A questionnaire is kind of a form, with algorithmic capabilities.

tags.name: questions
tags.description: Questions are part of a questionnaire, either belonging directly to it or shared between multiple questionnaires.
